# Cargo acceptance fixtures

Each subdirectory is a self-contained Cargo project that exercises a specific
shape the plugin should handle. The acceptance suite (`TestAcceptance` in
`acceptance_test.go`) runs the plugin against each fixture and asserts the
produced dep graph matches the committed golden JSON.

## Required structure

Every fixture directory must contain:

- `Cargo.toml` — the project (or workspace) manifest.
- `Cargo.lock` — generated by `cargo generate-lockfile` inside the fixture.
- `expected.json` — the golden dep graph for single-crate fixtures.
- `expected-<member>.json` — one per workspace member for workspace fixtures.

Fixtures missing `Cargo.lock` or any `expected*.json` are skipped silently by
the test discovery.

## Generating a new fixture

From the fixture directory:

```sh
cargo generate-lockfile          # creates Cargo.lock
cargo fetch                      # populates ~/.cargo/registry so the suite can run offline-ish
go test -v ./pkg/ecosystems/rust/cargo/... -run TestAcceptance -update
```

The `-update` flag regenerates the `expected*.json` files from the current
plugin output. Review the diff carefully before committing — that's the
oracle the test will pin against from then on.

## Fixtures to add

The plan calls for the following coverage. Add as needed; the suite handles
any subset.

- `simple` — one direct registry dep.
- `dev-deps` — `[dev-dependencies]`; verifies the IncludeDev flag.
- `build-deps` — `[build-dependencies]`; verifies build edges are walked.
- `multiple-versions` — two majors of the same crate coexist in the graph.
- `proc-macro` — a procedural macro dep (e.g. `serde_derive`).
- `workspaces` — standard workspace with multiple members.
- `workspaces-virtual` — virtual workspace (no root `[package]`).
- `workspaces-cross-dep` — member A depends on member B.
- `target-specific-deps` — `[target.'cfg(unix)'.dependencies]` and
  similar; verifies `--target=all` includes them.
- `out-of-sync-lockfile` — Cargo.toml mutated after Cargo.lock was
  generated; verifies `--locked` rejection and the friendly error message.

## Running without cargo

The acceptance suite calls `requireCargo` and skips when cargo isn't in
PATH. CI environments without the Rust toolchain will simply log a skip.
Unit tests (parser, depgraph builder, plugin orchestration) cover the
plugin's logic without needing cargo.
